title: rosa villosa subsp. tomentosa
intro: Rosa villosa subsp. tomentosa, also known as the hairy wild rose, is a species of rose native to the Americas, characterized by its prickly stems and fragrant flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa villosa subsp. tomentosa include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Rosa villosa subsp. tomentosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
